title: Restore reads an unbounded Blob stream before enforcing the byte cap when metadata size is unavailable

evidence.quote:
const text = await new Response(result.stream).text();
    if (Buffer.byteLength(text, "utf8") > MAX_RESTORE_BLOB_BYTES) {
it("rejects an oversized Blob after reading when metadata size is unavailable", async () => {

reasoning: The route only rejects by Blob metadata size before reading. If `result.blob.size` is `null`, the fallback uses `new Response(result.stream).text()`, which buffers the entire stream in memory before checking `MAX_RESTORE_BLOB_BYTES`. The included test explicitly models unavailable metadata, but it only spies on `Buffer.byteLength`; it does not assert that stream consumption is bounded. Because `pathname` is user-controlled within the allowlist and the Blob read is a network boundary, a large matching private Blob can consume memory and function time before the 413 response is produced.
reproduction: Create or mock a private Blob stream matching `address-labels-backup-YYYY-MM-DD.json` with `blob.size === null` and a body larger than `MAX_REDIS_HASH_REPLACE_BYTES`; POST with valid auth. The handler buffers the whole response body before returning 413.
recommendation: Replace `new Response(result.stream).text()` with a bounded stream reader that accumulates chunks up to `MAX_RESTORE_BLOB_BYTES + 1`, cancels the reader once the limit is exceeded, and returns 413 without buffering the rest. Apply a read timeout or abort controller to stream consumption as well as the initial Blob `get`.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The existing oversized-null-metadata test proves the fallback returns 413 after reading, but it mocks `Buffer.byteLength` and does not use a large or streaming body, so it cannot catch unbounded buffering or lack of cancellation.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a test with a custom `ReadableStream` whose `blob.size` is `null`, records how many chunks are pulled, and exceeds `MAX_REDIS_HASH_REPLACE_BYTES`; assert the handler returns 413 and cancels/stops reading immediately after the limit is crossed.
minimumFixScope: `ui-dashboard/src/app/api/address-labels/restore/route.ts` stream-reading logic plus the restore route test for null-size oversized streams.

history.note: Fixed by replacing Response(stream).text() with bounded stream reading that cancels once the restore cap is exceeded; added null-size stream regression.
